untranslatable_reason: Signal requires microstructure-specific inputs not available in standard OHLCV indicators: (1) 'Dealer ask price discount vs fundamental value' - fundamental value estimation requires proprietary model not in allowed indicators; (2) 'Available size for purchase' - order book depth data not in allowed indicators; (3) 'Dealer quote tightening detection' - requires bid-ask spread tracking and dealer identity classification not available. Approximated using price-to-SMA deviation, Bollinger bandwidth expansion, and volume ratio as proxies for inventory imbalance and spread widening, but this does not capture the true dealer microstructure mechanics.
translation_notes: Original signal is highly dealer-centric and requires real-time order book microstructure data. Attempted proxy mapping: (1) 'Dealer ask discount' → close < sma_20 (price below moving average as discount proxy); (2) 'Spread widening >2σ' → bb_bandwidth > 2.0 (Bollinger bandwidth captures volatility-driven widening, though not classical spread measurement); (3) 'Volume spike from dealers' → vol_ratio_20 > 1.5 (volume ratio above normal as activity spike proxy). Exit uses simple mean reversion cross. Hold period set to 0.25 days (15 minutes as stated). This is a degraded approximation suitable only for testing directional logic, not for actual dealer inventory capture.
